rationale: This structure is organized around major, distinct academic disciplines that practitioners identify with. Core fields like History and Philosophy, which are too broad, have been split into thematic subfields. This split prioritizes areas with strong connections to scientific methods and topics, such as History of Science and Philosophy of Science, to best serve the app's audience. I chose not to split fields like Archaeology or Classics further, as these names represent cohesive professional identities and further division would create categories with too small an audience. The inclusion of Linguistics and Religious Studies acknowledges their strong methodological overlap with the core humanities, even though they are sometimes considered separate faculties.

subspecialties.name: History of Science & Technology
subspecialties.tagline: The development of scientific thought, practices, and technologies over time.
covers: The history of specific scientific disciplines (e.g., physics, biology, medicine)
The role of technology in societal and environmental change
The social and institutional history of science
explicitly_excludes: Philosophy of science → Philosophy of Science
Sociology of science → Social & Cultural History

subspecialties.name: Philosophy of Science
subspecialties.tagline: The foundations, methods, and implications of scientific theories and practice.
covers: The nature of scientific explanation, evidence, and causation
Realism, reductionism, and the structure of scientific theories
Philosophy of specific sciences like biology, physics, or cognitive science
explicitly_excludes: History of scientific ideas → History of Science & Technology
General epistemology → Epistemology & Metaphysics

subspecialties.name: Archaeology
subspecialties.tagline: Investigating the human past through material remains and environmental data.
covers: Excavation reports, artifact analysis, and chronometric dating techniques
Bioarchaeology, zooarchaeology, and archaeobotany
Computational archaeology, remote sensing, and cultural heritage management
explicitly_excludes: Analysis of ancient historical texts → Classics or Ancient History
Human evolution and primatology → Biological Anthropology (in Social Sciences)

subspecialties.name: Linguistics
subspecialties.tagline: The scientific study of language structure, meaning, use, and change.
covers: Phonetics, phonology, syntax, and semantics
Historical linguistics, language evolution, and typology
Corpus linguistics and quantitative analysis of language data
explicitly_excludes: Natural Language Processing for AI → Computer Science & AI
Philosophy of language → Philosophy of Mind & Language

subspecialties.name: Philosophy of Mind & Language
subspecialties.tagline: Exploring consciousness, intentionality, meaning, and communication.
covers: The mind-body problem and theories of consciousness
The nature of meaning, reference, and truth
Cognition, representation, and conceptual analysis of AI
explicitly_excludes: Empirical psychology → Psychology (in Social Sciences)
Formal linguistic structure → Linguistics

subspecialties.name: Social & Cultural History
subspecialties.tagline: The study of everyday life, social structures, and cultural practices in the past.
covers: History of family, gender, race, and class
Popular culture, rituals, and belief systems
Microhistory, environmental history, and historical anthropology
explicitly_excludes: Political events and institutions → Political & Economic History
Contemporary sociological studies → Sociology (in Social Sciences)

subspecialties.name: Ethics & Political Philosophy
subspecialties.tagline: The study of moral principles, justice, rights, and government.
covers: Normative ethics, meta-ethics, and applied ethics (e.g., bioethics, tech ethics)
Theories of justice, liberty, and political obligation
Analysis of political ideologies and human rights
explicitly_excludes: Empirical political science → Political Science (in Social Sciences)
History of political thought → Political & Economic History

subspecialties.name: Political & Economic History
subspecialties.tagline: Examining states, institutions, economies, and power dynamics in the past.
covers: History of governments, wars, and international relations
Economic systems, trade, labor, and business history
The development of political and economic institutions
explicitly_excludes: Social movements and daily life → Social & Cultural History
Contemporary economic modeling → Economics (in Social Sciences)

subspecialties.name: Art History
subspecialties.tagline: The study of visual arts in their historical and cultural contexts.
covers: Analysis of painting, sculpture, architecture, and other media
Technical art history, including materials analysis and conservation science
Iconography, patronage, and the theory of art
explicitly_excludes: Analysis of archaeological artifacts for cultural history → Archaeology
Aesthetics as a branch of philosophy → Epistemology & Metaphysics

subspecialties.name: Ancient History
subspecialties.tagline: The study of societies from early writing to the post-classical era.
covers: History of Mesopotamia, Egypt, Greece, Rome, and other ancient civilizations
Analysis of historical sources, inscriptions, and papyri
Warfare, politics, and society in the ancient world
explicitly_excludes: Pre-literate societies → Archaeology
Literary and philological analysis of Greek/Roman texts → Classics

subspecialties.name: Classics
subspecialties.tagline: The study of the languages, literature, and culture of ancient Greece and Rome.
covers: Philological and linguistic analysis of Greek and Latin texts
Ancient literature, drama, and rhetoric
Reception studies of the classical world in later eras
explicitly_excludes: Archaeology of Greece and Rome → Archaeology
Purely historical analysis of events in Greece and Rome → Ancient History

subspecialties.name: Literary Studies
subspecialties.tagline: The analysis of literature using empirical, quantitative, or cognitive methods.
covers: Quantitative analysis of literary style (stylometry)
Corpus-based studies of literary history and genre
Evolutionary and cognitive approaches to narrative and poetry
explicitly_excludes: Traditional literary criticism and post-structuralist theory
Development of NLP models → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Epistemology & Metaphysics
subspecialties.tagline: The study of knowledge, belief, reality, and the nature of being.
covers: Theories of justification, truth, and belief
The nature of existence, time, causality, and modality
Ontology and the fundamental structure of reality
explicitly_excludes: Philosophy of science specifically → Philosophy of Science
Philosophy of mind → Philosophy of Mind & Language

subspecialties.name: Religious Studies
subspecialties.tagline: The academic study of religious beliefs, behaviors, and institutions.
covers: History of world religions and comparative mythology
Cognitive science of religion and ritual studies
Sociological and anthropological approaches to religion
explicitly_excludes: Theology and confessional (faith-based) studies
History of specific religious conflicts → Political & Economic History

uncovered_concerns: Area Studies (e.g., East Asian Studies) were excluded in favor of disciplinary categories, which better fit a scientific paper structure.
A broader 'Digital Humanities' category was considered but omitted to avoid overlap with the CS & AI category, per instructions.
Musicology and Performance Studies were excluded as they represent a smaller fraction of empirical papers in typical science databases.
The 'Literary Studies' category is narrowly defined to empirical methods, excluding the bulk of traditional literary criticism to adhere to the prompt's scope.
